# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 351
- **Lines of code**: 82,658
- **Languages**: TypeScript (280), JSON (22), CSS (16), Other (10), Markdown (10)
- **Analyzed**: 2026-02-05T17:19:43.947Z

## Architecture Zones

### Hench Completion (`hench-completion`)

Completion detection and validation logic for determining when agent tasks are finished.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.57, Coupling: 0.43
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/completion.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-completion/context.md`

### Hench Core (`hench-core`)

Main autonomous agent package containing the agent loop, briefing system, tool implementations, and run management.

- Files: 49
- Cohesion: 0.95, Coupling: 0.05
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/schema/index.ts, packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts, packages/hench/src/tools/rex.ts, packages/hench/src/types/index.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-core/context.md`

### Hench Guard (`hench-guard`)

Security guard layer providing command validation, path restrictions, and safe tool execution for the agent.

- Files: 12
- Cohesion: 0.80, Coupling: 0.20
- Entry points: packages/hench/src/agent/tools.ts, packages/hench/src/guard/index.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/hench-guard/context.md`

### Rex PRD Management CLI (`packages-rex:rex-cli`)

A self-contained CLI tool and MCP server for managing hierarchical product requirements documents, including code analysis, tree operations, validation, persistence, and AI-agent workflow integration.

- Files: 36
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-rex:rex-cli/context.md`

### Analysis Engine (`packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine`)

Core analysis pipeline including file inventory, import graph extraction, Louvain community detection, route parsing, AI enrichment, and CLI orchestration.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.94, Coupling: 0.06
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/components.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/imports.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/inventory.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/analyzers/zones.ts, packages/sourcevision/src/schema/data-files.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:analysis-engine/context.md`

### Interactive Viewer (`packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer`)

Preact single-page application that visualizes analysis results through force-directed graphs, zone maps, route trees, and findings panels, consuming only schema types with zero analyzer dependencies.

- Files: 46
- Cohesion: 0.96, Coupling: 0.04
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:interactive-viewer/context.md`

### Test Fixtures (`packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures`)

Self-contained sample TypeScript project used as input data for integration and end-to-end tests, intentionally isolated with no imports from or exports to production code.

- Files: 7
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-fixtures/context.md`

### Test Suite (`packages-sourcevision:test-suite`)

Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests covering analyzer logic, schema validation, CLI workflows, and the serve command, with imports reaching into both analyzer and viewer zones.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.46, Coupling: 0.54
- Entry points: packages/sourcevision/src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/packages-sourcevision:test-suite/context.md`

### Root Orchestration (`root-orchestration`)

Top-level CLI entry points and configuration for the n-dx monorepo, delegating to individual packages.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/root-orchestration/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `packages/rex/src/schema/index.ts` (76 imports)
- `packages/sourcevision/src/schema/index.ts` (31 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/core/tree.ts` (26 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/store/index.ts` (22 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/cli/commands/constants.ts` (19 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/cli/output.ts` (19 imports)
- `packages/sourcevision/src/schema/v1.ts` (19 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/cli/errors.ts` (18 imports)
- `packages/hench/src/schema/v1.ts` (15 imports)
- `packages/rex/src/core/canonical.ts` (14 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 119 files)
- `preact` (used by 25 files)
- `rex` (used by 11 files)
- `zod` (used by 7 files)
- `@anthropic-ai/sdk` (used by 3 files)
- `typescript` (used by 3 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `child_process` (used by 2 files)
- `path` (used by 2 files)
- `url` (used by 2 files)

## Route Structure

### Route Tree

```
/ → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/_index.tsx
/users/:id → packages/sourcevision/tests/fixtures/remix-app/app/routes/users.$id.tsx
```

### Convention Exports

- action: 2 modules
- default: 4 modules
- loader: 2 modules
- meta: 1 modules

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- 49-file core zone vs 3-file completion zone (16:1 ratio) suggests zone detection algorithm parameters may need tuning or manual zone hints [info]
- Triangle dependency (core ↔ completion ↔ guard ↔ core) prevents independent testing and deployment of zones — extract shared types into dedicated interface zone [warning]
- Test file completion-validation.test.ts tests tools/ code but lives in completion zone — zone boundary is incorrectly drawn or test should move [info] (hench-completion)
- Schema types exported from core create implicit dependency for all consumers — should be isolated in dedicated types/schema zone to break coupling [warning] (hench-core)
- Zone contains 49 files spanning agent logic, storage, schema, CLI, and utilities — violates single responsibility; extract storage and schema into separate zones [warning] (hench-core)
- Path validation (paths.ts) and command validation (commands.ts) share no common interface despite both being validators — missing PolicyValidator abstraction [info] (hench-guard)
- tools.ts in agent/ directory imports guard but guard/commands.ts imports types from agent — circular dependency path requires interface extraction [warning] (hench-guard)

### Patterns

- Hench package zones would benefit from an explicit 'contracts' or 'interfaces' zone to break bidirectional dependencies between core↔guard and core↔completion [warning]
- Agent lifecycle files (brief→loop→review→summary) form a linear pipeline but lack explicit phase boundaries in the type system [info] (hench-core)
- Path and command validation split across multiple files enables granular testing but requires careful coordination of allow/deny list updates [info] (hench-guard)
- Orchestration layer delegates entirely through CLI spawning rather than direct imports, creating a process boundary that prevents accidental tight coupling [info] (root-orchestration)

### Relationships

- Cross-zone import counts (6+4+2+1=13 total) are low relative to file count (67 files) indicating generally good encapsulation despite bidirectional issues [info]
- Completion depends on core agent state types but core also imports completion utilities — circular dependency suggests completion checks should be injected rather than imported [warning] (hench-completion)
- Core zone exports types consumed by both guard and completion zones but has reverse dependencies on both, suggesting missing abstraction layer for shared contracts [warning] (hench-core)
- Security policy enforcement (guard) importing from protected code (core tools) creates potential for policy bypass if tool implementations change [warning] (hench-guard)

### Observations

- Bidirectional coupling: "hench-completion" ↔ "hench-core" (1+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "hench-core" ↔ "hench-guard" (4+6 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 49 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Contains 53% of project files (49/93) — may be too broad, consider splitting [warning] (hench-core)
- High cohesion (0.95) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-core)
- High cohesion (0.8) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (hench-guard)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (root-orchestration)
- All zones show healthy cohesion/coupling ratios indicating good architectural boundaries [info]
- No circular dependency concerns detected in cross-zone import patterns [info]
- hench-completion's small size (3 files) and high coupling suggests it may not need to be a separate zone [warning]
- ... and 10 more

### Suggestions

- Hench package lacks a root barrel file (src/index.ts) — external consumers must import from internal paths like src/schema/index.ts [warning]
- Zone detection found rex and sourcevision packages but assigned no zones — verify those packages have sufficient internal imports for community detection threshold [info]
- completion-validation.test.ts tests tools/ code but lives in completion zone — file may be misplaced or indicates completion logic leaked into tools layer [warning] (hench-completion)
- src/agent/ mixes lifecycle stages (brief→loop→review→summary) with cross-cutting concerns (stuck, token-budget) — subdirectory could benefit from further organization [info] (hench-core)
- token-budget.ts has single consumer (loop.ts) — consider inlining to reduce module count unless reuse is planned [info] (hench-core)
- paths.ts and commands.ts define parallel security policies without a common Policy interface — opportunity to unify validation patterns [info] (hench-guard)
- tools.ts imports from guard/commands.ts directly instead of through guard/index.ts barrel — creates implicit coupling that bypasses the public API [warning] (hench-guard)
